title: Correction loop stops at the admin-configured maximum iteration count
preconditions: Admin has set the correction loop maximum to 3 iterations
A content section is in review and requires correction
The correction agent is configured to always request further correction
steps: Trigger the content correction loop for the section
Allow the loop to run until it terminates
Retrieve the iteration count from the correction run record
expected_result: The correction loop terminates after exactly 3 iterations. No fourth iteration is attempted.

title: Correction loop does not execute beyond the configured limit
preconditions: Admin has set the correction loop maximum to 3 iterations
A content section correction loop has already completed 3 iterations without success
steps: Attempt to trigger a 4th correction iteration manually
Observe the agent response
expected_result: The agent refuses to execute the 4th iteration and returns an error indicating the loop cap has been reached.

title: Correction loop terminates exactly at the limit boundary
preconditions: Admin has set the correction loop maximum to 1 iteration
steps: Trigger a correction loop for a section that will not pass on the first attempt
Observe whether a second iteration is started
expected_result: The correction loop executes exactly 1 iteration and stops. No second iteration is initiated, confirming a limit of 1 is respected.

title: Admin receives escalation notification when correction loop is exhausted
preconditions: Admin has set the correction loop maximum to 2 iterations
A content section correction loop has completed 2 iterations without producing passing content
An admin notification channel is configured and observable
steps: Allow the correction loop to exhaust its iterations
Check the admin notification channel for an escalation message
expected_result: An escalation notification is delivered to the admin channel containing the course ID, section ID, and iteration count at time of exhaustion.

title: No admin escalation is sent when correction loop succeeds within the limit
preconditions: Admin has set the correction loop maximum to 5 iterations
A content section correction loop succeeds on the 2nd iteration
An admin notification channel is configured and observable
steps: Allow the correction loop to run until the section passes
Check the admin notification channel for any escalation messages
expected_result: No escalation notification is delivered to the admin channel. The correction run record shows status succeeded.

title: Escalation notification is sent even when loop limit is set to 1
preconditions: Admin has set the correction loop maximum to 1 iteration
A content section correction fails on the first and only attempt
steps: Trigger the correction loop for the failing section
Check the admin notification channel after the single iteration completes
expected_result: An escalation notification is delivered to the admin channel after the single failed iteration, confirming escalation fires even at the minimum cap.

title: Homework due dates are calculated from the approved course syllabus
preconditions: A student has approved a syllabus specifying homework due 7 days after each lesson
The syllabus approval timestamp is recorded
steps: Allow content generation to complete
Retrieve the due date for the first homework assignment
Compare it to the expected date derived from the syllabus schedule
expected_result: The first homework due date is exactly 7 calendar days after the lesson start date specified in the syllabus, confirming syllabus-driven calculation.

title: Homework due dates are not generated for an unapproved syllabus
preconditions: A student has a generated syllabus in pending-approval state
steps: Query the homework due date records for the student's course
expected_result: No homework due date records exist for the course, confirming due dates are only assigned after syllabus approval.

title: Due dates for all homework assignments span the full syllabus schedule
preconditions: A student has approved a syllabus containing exactly 4 homework assignments spread across 4 weeks
steps: Allow content generation to complete
Retrieve all homework due date records for the course
Verify count and chronological ordering
expected_result: Exactly 4 homework due date records exist, each assigned to a different week, ordered chronologically from earliest to latest.

title: Section content is regenerated when student submits a change request
preconditions: A course section has been generated and delivered to the student
The student has not yet submitted feedback for the section
steps: Student submits a change request for the section with feedback 'Please include more examples'
Poll the section status until it reaches a regenerated state
Retrieve the new section content
expected_result: The section record shows a new version with updated content. The previous version is preserved. The new content differs from the original.

title: Section regeneration is rejected when no change request is submitted
preconditions: A course section has been generated and delivered to the student
steps: Trigger section regeneration for the section without providing any student feedback
Observe the agent response
expected_result: The agent returns an error indicating that a student change request is required before section regeneration can proceed.

title: Section regeneration preserves the original section version
preconditions: A course section has been generated
The original content hash is recorded
steps: Student submits a change request for the section
Wait for regeneration to complete
Retrieve the version history for the section
expected_result: The section version history contains at least two entries: the original version and the regenerated version. The original content hash is unchanged in the historical record.

title: Content generation halts when an AI API call exhausts all retries
preconditions: Admin has configured 2 retries before halt
The AI API is configured to return a non-retryable error on every call
A student's content generation pipeline is in progress
steps: Allow the generation pipeline to attempt an AI API call
Observe the pipeline state after all retries are exhausted
expected_result: The content generation pipeline transitions to a halted state. No further generation tasks are started. The halt reason references API failure.

title: Content generation continues when AI API call succeeds within retry limit
preconditions: Admin has configured 3 retries
The AI API is configured to fail on the first call and succeed on the second
A student's content generation pipeline is in progress
steps: Allow the generation pipeline to attempt and retry the AI API call
Observe the pipeline state after the successful retry
expected_result: The pipeline does not halt. Content generation continues and the pipeline reaches a non-halted state after the successful retry.

title: Partial content is not delivered to student when pipeline halts on API failure
preconditions: Admin has configured 1 retry
The AI API fails after generating 1 of 3 lessons
A student's content generation pipeline is in progress
steps: Allow the pipeline to halt after the API failure
Check the student-facing course content view
expected_result: The student-facing view shows the pipeline as halted and does not present the partially generated lesson as complete course content.

title: Retry delays increase exponentially after consecutive rate-limit responses
preconditions: The agent retry scheduler uses an injectable clock and delay recorder
The AI API is stubbed to return rate-limit responses for the first 3 calls
steps: Trigger an AI API call that receives 3 consecutive rate-limit responses
Record the delay applied before each retry attempt
Verify the delay sequence
expected_result: Each recorded delay is greater than the previous delay. The ratio between consecutive delays is consistent with a configured exponential base (e.g., delay_n = base^n * initial_delay).

title: No retry delay is applied after a successful AI API call
preconditions: The agent retry scheduler uses an injectable clock
The AI API is stubbed to return success on the first call
steps: Trigger an AI API call that succeeds immediately
Check whether any retry delay was recorded
expected_result: No retry delay is recorded. The call succeeds on the first attempt without any backoff being applied.

title: Generation halts after max retries are exhausted due to rate limiting
preconditions: Admin has configured max retries to 3
The AI API is stubbed to always return rate-limit responses
The agent retry scheduler uses an injectable clock
steps: Trigger an AI API call
Allow the backoff loop to exhaust all 3 retries
Observe the final outcome
expected_result: After 3 failed retries the agent stops attempting and returns a rate-limit exhaustion error. Exactly 3 backoff delays are recorded before termination.

title: All in-flight generation operations are terminated before account deletion executes
preconditions: A student has an active content generation pipeline in progress
An admin has initiated a deletion request for the student account
steps: Observe the agent module response to the deletion signal
Verify all active generation tasks for the student reach a terminated state
Verify the account deletion then proceeds
expected_result: All in-flight generation tasks show a terminated status before the account deletion completes. No orphaned generation tasks remain after deletion.

title: Account deletion does not proceed while generation tasks are still active
preconditions: A student has an active content generation pipeline in progress
The termination signal to the agent module is intentionally delayed
steps: Initiate account deletion without waiting for agent termination to complete
Attempt to read the deletion completion timestamp before generation tasks have terminated
expected_result: The account deletion is not marked complete while any generation task for the student remains in an active state.

title: Safe termination completes successfully when no generation is in flight
preconditions: A student has no active generation tasks
An admin has initiated a deletion request for the student account
steps: Send the termination signal to the agent module for the student
Observe the termination response
expected_result: The agent module acknowledges termination immediately with a success response and zero tasks terminated, allowing deletion to proceed.

title: Timeout is detected when content generation exceeds the configured time limit
preconditions: Admin has configured the content generation timeout to 5 seconds
The AI API is stubbed to respond after 10 seconds
A student's content generation pipeline is in progress
steps: Trigger content generation for a lesson
Wait for the timeout period to elapse
Inspect the pipeline run record for a timeout event
expected_result: The pipeline run record contains a timeout event with a timestamp within the configured timeout window. The generation task is marked as timed out.

title: No timeout event is recorded when generation completes within the time limit
preconditions: Admin has configured the content generation timeout to 30 seconds
The AI API is stubbed to respond in 2 seconds
A student's content generation pipeline is in progress
steps: Trigger content generation for a lesson
Wait for generation to complete
Inspect the pipeline run record for timeout events
expected_result: No timeout event is present in the pipeline run record. The generation task shows a completed status.

title: Timeout is detected when generation completes exactly at the limit boundary
preconditions: Admin has configured the content generation timeout to 10 seconds
The agent module uses an injectable clock
The stubbed AI response arrives at exactly 10 seconds elapsed
steps: Trigger content generation with the injectable clock at T=0
Advance the clock to exactly 10 seconds
Observe whether a timeout event is recorded
expected_result: A timeout event is recorded when elapsed time equals the configured limit, confirming the boundary is treated as a timeout condition.

title: Student receives a response to a natural language question via the chat interface
preconditions: A student has an active course
The chat interface endpoint is available
steps: Student sends the message 'Can you explain what a mutex is?' via the chat interface
Wait for the agent response
expected_result: The chat interface returns a natural language response to the student's question. The response is non-empty and pertains to the topic of mutexes.

title: Chat interface rejects messages from unauthenticated users
preconditions: No authenticated session exists
steps: Send a chat message to the chat interface endpoint without an authentication token
Observe the HTTP response
expected_result: The endpoint returns a 401 Unauthorized response. No AI processing is performed.

title: Chat interface maintains context across multiple messages in a session
preconditions: A student has an active course
The chat interface endpoint is available
steps: Student sends 'My topic is sorting algorithms'
Wait for agent acknowledgment
Student sends 'What did I just tell you my topic was?'
Observe the agent response
expected_result: The agent's second response correctly identifies 'sorting algorithms' as the topic the student provided, confirming conversational context is maintained within the session.
